prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed using the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill with the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (xtb engine), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). The N+1 (anion) and N−1 (cation) states were calculated at charge ±1, multiplicity 2 (doublet) respectively. Partial charges used the Mulliken (GFN2-xTB) scheme. The geometry was taken as-supplied from ferrocene.xyz with no re-optimization. All three SCF runs converged. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true; sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported.

Headline result: The iron centre (Fe, atom 11 of 21) is the dominant reactive site across all Fukui descriptors. Most nucleophilic site: Fe with f⁻ = 0.25938191. Most electrophilic site: Fe with f⁺ = 0.22885456. The dual descriptor on Fe is −0.030527354, indicating net nucleophilic character consistent with Fe(II)'s filled d-orbitals. All 10 cyclopentadienyl carbons are symmetry-equivalent (f⁻ ≈ 0.01218, f⁺ ≈ 0.01364) and the 10 H atoms show modest uniform response (f ≈ 0.062). Method reference: Bannwarth, C.; Ehlert, S.; Grimme, S. J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
